expect: H1: Rolling-window LR mean range >0.3 (attractor drifts upward). H2: Within-regime ceiling proximity predicts transitions with >60% precision.
actual: H1 CONFIRMED: attractor drifts at +0.005 Sharpe/session (0.50/100 sessions, R²=0.63). Trend+OU wins BIC (-85.64) over fixed OU (115.15) and linear (-80.61). Detrended beta=0.460 (mean-reversion preserved around drifting trend). H2 PARTIAL: n=2 transitions insufficient (1/2 elevated, 50%). Ceiling prediction underpowered, not falsified.
diff: Expected attractor drift >0.3: actual range 3.795 (12.7x pre-registered threshold). Expected ceiling precision >60%: got 50% but n=2 (underpowered). Key surprise: (1) Beta is VARIABLE across windows (-0.12 to 0.72) — fixed-OU assumption from S538 is window-dependent. (2) Trend+OU model unifies L-1605 (oscillation) with L-1600 (growth): quality BOTH improves long-term AND oscillates short-term. (3) L-1605 prediction (decline to 8.97 by S574) is still plausible because drift+reversion converge near that value.

predictions.falsification: If S574 mean < 8.5, attractor drift model falsified

h2_ceiling_prediction.note: n=2 transitions insufficient for statistical test. Ceiling prediction underpowered, not falsified.

synthesis.unified_model: Quality follows OU process around linearly drifting attractor: μ(t) = 6.09 + 0.005t, with beta=0.46 mean-reversion and σ=0.204 noise
synthesis.relationship_to_l1605: L-1605 is first-order approximation (fixed attractor). This adds time-varying mean. Both agree on short-term prediction but diverge long-term.
synthesis.relationship_to_l1600: L-1600 identified deceleration (0.42x growth slowdown). Drift rate 0.005/session is the residual growth after deceleration — slow but persistent.
synthesis.challenge: L-1605 claims quality caps at ~8.8. Drift model predicts slow continued improvement to ~9.6 by S700. Testable: if S700 mean still > 9.2, fixed-attractor model falsified.
